rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption - Liu Only
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, Line 8, 1- PLAINTIFF FLICK, INC.'s Hearing On Claim Of Exemption - Liu Only. Before the court is judgment debtor's claim of exemption. Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HALL ET AL VS. VELODYNE LIDAR, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, Line 3. 2 - PLAINTIFFs DAVID HALL and MARTA HALL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production (Set One). The court concludes that exceptional circumstances exist and appointment of a discovery referee is warranted for all discovery purposes. (CCP 639(a)(5); CRC 3.920(c).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Plaintiffs David and Marta Hall, were board members of Defendant Velodyne Lidar, Inc. Shareholders sued Defendant and Plaintiff in a shareholder derivative action. Plaintiffs incurred attorney fees during their defense. Plaintiffs now sue defendants for indemnification for those fees. Specifically, Plaintiffs allege breach of contract and violation of labor code section 2802, which they assert requires reimbursement for employees' necessary expenditures. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: JEANINE OLSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Production And Requests For Admission; Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Production And Requests For Admission; Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Request For Sanctions. Defendant San Francisco Opera Association's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide code compliant responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set No. 1, Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1, Defendant's Request for Production of Documents, Set No. 1 without objections no later than 10 days from notice of entry of this order. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Defendant San Francisco Opera Association's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses Requests For Admission and to Deem Requests For Admission Admitted is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The requests for admissions set out in Defendant's Request for Admissions, Set No. 1 are deemed admitted. Plaintiff has not demonstrated her failure to respond was reasonable or justified. The request for responses to the request for admission is denied as moot, given that the requests are deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion To Disqualify, Motion To Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, Line 5, PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL's Motion To Disqualify, Motion To Sanction. Plaintiff Daniel Powell's Motion To Disqualify and Motion To Sanction is DENIED. Plaintiff seeks an order disqualifying deputy city attorney Jose Zelidon-Zepeda from representing Defendants in this action. Plaintiff has not shown a factual or legal basis for such order. Indeed, Penal Code section 1424 has no application to this civil case and there is no factual basis for removal pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 or section 128.7. The motion, thus, is denied. Plaintiff seeks an order sanctioning counsel Zelidon-Zepeda. Plaintiff has not shown a factual or legal basis for such order. Penal Code section 1424 has no application to this civil case and there is no factual basis for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 or section 128.7. The motion, thus, is denied. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. USA DENTAL SOLUTIONS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Second) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order To which no opposition has been received is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Moving Party needs to explain in the moving papers, specifically the memorandum of points and authorities, why it no requires an order substantially identical to the order the court signed and filed on December 5, 2024. The only apparent difference between the December 5, 2024 order and the current proposed order is the shorthand by which the Judgment Debtor refers to the Judgment Creditors, which, of course, is without legal effect. If somehow this difference has legal effect, this must be explained in writing. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
